description:
A database-administrator has asked you to clean up a scattered set of
  SQL-backup files and produce an auditable report.

  1. Starting point  
     • A project tree already exists at  
       /home/user/db_migrations/  
       with many sub-directories that contain “*.sql.bak” files.  
     • DO NOT create or delete the directory tree itself; only reorganise
       the “*.sql.bak” files that meet the size criterion described below.

  2. Scope of the clean-up  
     • Any file whose name ends with “.sql.bak”  
     • whose *actual* size is strictly larger than 1024 bytes  
       (use the file’s byte size, not blocks, KB, etc.).  
     • These qualifying files must be gathered, compressed
       and logged; non-qualifying files are left untouched.

  3. Required end state  
     a. A new directory must exist:  
        /home/user/db_migrations/backup_large  
        (create it if absent; permissions default to umask).  

     b. Every qualifying “.sql.bak” file must be:  
        • moved into that directory,  
        • gzip-compressed in-place so the final file name becomes  
          <original-name>.gz (the “.bak” suffix remains, so
          “foo.sql.bak” → “foo.sql.bak.gz”).  

     c. A plaintext audit file must be produced at  
        /home/user/db_migrations/backup_large/operation.log  

        Format requirements (exactly, one line per processed file):  
           ORIGINAL_BASENAME|SIZE_IN_BYTES|GZ_FILENAME  
        • ORIGINAL_BASENAME – just the file name, no path.  
        • SIZE_IN_BYTES    – the integer byte size *before* compression.  
        • GZ_FILENAME      – the resulting compressed file name
                             (also basename only).  
        • Lines must be sorted in ascending ASCII order
          by ORIGINAL_BASENAME.  
        • The file must end with a single ‘\n’. No extra blank lines.  

  4. Constraints / hints  
     • Accomplish the batch selection, relocation and compression
       using a *single* pipeline that combines find(1) and xargs(1)
       (-print0/-0 pairing is strongly recommended to handle any
       odd characters).  
     • Do not hard-code file names; the solution must work solely
       from the criteria above.  
     • After the task completes, only the compressed “.gz”
       versions of qualifying files should exist in
       /home/user/db_migrations/backup_large/, and the originals
       should no longer be present in their old locations.  
     • All other files/directories in /home/user/db_migrations/
       must remain exactly as they were.  

  5. What the automated checker will validate  
     • The directory /home/user/db_migrations/backup_large exists.  
     • It contains exactly the set of “*.sql.bak.gz” files whose
       *uncompressed* counterparts were >1024 bytes.  
     • Each listed file, when gunzipped, matches the source file’s
       recorded byte size.  
     • /home/user/db_migrations/backup_large/operation.log exists and
       conforms *precisely* to the format in 3c.  

  Produce no additional output files beyond those explicitly required.
truth:
Initial files pre-populated in the image:
  1. /home/user/db_migrations/2021/01_schema_update.sql.bak      — 1500 bytes (contents: “--01...A” repeated)
  2. /home/user/db_migrations/2021/02_index_update.sql.bak       —  900 bytes
  3. /home/user/db_migrations/2022/03_add_columns.sql.bak        — 3000 bytes
  4. /home/user/db_migrations/2022/temp/04_cleanup.sql.bak       — 5000 bytes
  5. /home/user/db_migrations/2023/05_remove_obsolete.sql.bak    —  800 bytes
  6. /home/user/db_migrations/legacy/06_legacy_patch.sql.bak     — 2048 bytes

  Files qualifying (>1024 bytes): #1, #3, #4, #6.

  Required final compressed files (all under /home/user/db_migrations/backup_large/):
    01_schema_update.sql.bak.gz
    03_add_columns.sql.bak.gz
    04_cleanup.sql.bak.gz
    06_legacy_patch.sql.bak.gz

  Content of /home/user/db_migrations/backup_large/operation.log
  (final newline after last line, no extra blank lines):

    01_schema_update.sql.bak|1500|01_schema_update.sql.bak.gz
    03_add_columns.sql.bak|3000|03_add_columns.sql.bak.gz
    04_cleanup.sql.bak|5000|04_cleanup.sql.bak.gz
    06_legacy_patch.sql.bak|2048|06_legacy_patch.sql.bak.gz

  The original uncompressed files #1, #3, #4, #6 must no longer exist
  in their old paths after the agent’s actions. Non-qualifying files
  (#2, #5) remain untouched.
